rationale.R1.1: The delegation asks to make the research framing stronger, which centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the passage requires checking that the motivation, prior-work contrast, method description, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims about benchmark performance, so strengthening includes ensuring those claims align with the reported results.
rationale.R1.4: The text contains a strong novelty claim about being the first algorithm, making novelty positioning central to the requested improvement.
rationale.R1.5: The artifact is a compact research introduction or abstract, so making it stronger can involve section- or paragraph-level narrative ordering.
rationale.R1.6: The broad request naturally includes improving sentence-level force, concision, flow, and academic register.
rationale.R1.7: Because the artifact names specific prior work and distinguishes related methods, citation coverage and attribution are active responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad delegation must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact includes potentially strong claims, so avoiding overstated novelty or performance implications is required.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must only project the delegation and not actually rewrite or evaluate the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid assigning responsibilities that could encourage misleading scientific claims or unsupported research positioning.
rationale.RX.5: The active responsibilities should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
